hero.description: I help businesses launch fast, modern, and scalable websites and platforms — from landing pages to complex React applications.
hero.card_description: I combine strong visual style, thoughtful architecture, and practical development so the project is beautiful, fast, and easy to scale.
hero.point_1: I design interface architecture for easy scalability.
hero.point_2: I work remotely and flexibly, so I can efficiently handle projects from anywhere.
hero.point_3: I create not just beautiful pages, but clear and functional digital products.

features.section_title: Approach that saves business time and money
features.section_subtitle: I create not just beautiful interfaces, but thoughtful solutions that are easy to launch, maintain, and scale further.
features.card1_desc: I write clean, modular, and understandable code so the project doesn't hit limitations just months after launch.
features.card2_desc: I optimize interfaces, loading speed, and project structure for fast performance and strong first impressions.
features.card3_desc: I create clean, intuitive, and visually strong interfaces that make product interaction simple and pleasant.
bottom_points: Clean development without chaos or overload.
Focus on results, not just visuals.
Approach convenient for future project growth.

contact.title: Let's discuss your project

pricing.section_title: Clear work formats, pricing, and timelines
pricing.section_subtitle: Below are the main directions I work with. Final cost and timeline depend on structure, functionality, and project complexity.
pricing.card1_desc: Perfect for personal brand, service, product, or clean project presentation in one strong screen or compact structure.
card1_points: Good option for launch without complexity.
pricing.card2_desc: For business, service, or company needing broader structure, multiple pages, thoughtful navigation, and scalability.
card2_points: Suitable for corporate and service websites.
pricing.card3_desc: For online stores, custom platforms, or specific business logic — project is estimated individually.
card3_points: Timeline and cost depend on scope.

faq.section_subtitle: I've collected answers to the most frequent questions that come up before beginning work.
faq.side_note: If you have a non-standard task or no ready technical specification, that's not a problem — we can discuss everything and determine the optimal work format together.
faq.cta_title: Didn't find the answer you need?
faq.cta_text: Write to me directly, and I'll help understand the best way to implement your project.

items.question: What is included in the base website price?
items.answer: Base price includes responsive website, one design variant, minor client revisions, and project deployment. Domain and hosting are not included — these expenses remain on the client side.

items.question: How many revisions are included?
items.answer: Base price includes 3 revision rounds. Each additional revision round is charged separately — 10€ per round.

items.question: What do I need to provide to start work?
items.answer: To start, it's best to prepare logo, brand colors, text, website examples, references, and approximate structure understanding: what sections or pages are needed. PDF format works best, but we can discuss everything in conversation if needed.

items.question: Can I reach out without ready technical specification?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely. If you don't have a ready specification, write to me — I'll help understand project structure, determine needed pages, and establish the basic direction to start.

items.question: Can I order an urgent project?
items.answer: Yes, urgent projects are possible if I have available capacity. Pricing is discussed separately taking urgency into account.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: Payment is split into two parts: 30% upfront before start and remaining amount after project completion and deployment.

path_page.title: My engineering path
path_page.subtitle: 6 years in development: from first code experiments to creating telemetry for industrial equipment and European logistics.
path_page.timeline_1_desc: First programming exposure in 11th grade through school Java project (JavaHamster equivalent). In 12th grade, self-studied web technologies and fundamentals: HTML/CSS/PHP and low-level C/C++/C#.
path_page.timeline_2_desc: Work at Austrian Schiebel Antriebstechnik. Joined mobile app development team on C# MAUI connecting to proprietary drives in real-time, displaying critical equipment parameters.
path_page.timeline_3_desc: Led Flutter app development for boat VESC motor monitoring. Real-time telemetry system: speed, GPS, motor characteristics. Full hardware integration.
path_page.timeline_4_desc: Maintaining and modernizing large ERP logistics system operating in 6 European countries. Stack: C# .NET 4.7.2, SQL Server, WebForms, CI/CD. Legacy optimization and tech migration.
path_page.timeline_5_desc: First client — father, then through friends and recommendations. Started with PHP, quickly moved to React for high-speed landing pages. Full cycle: design to deployment.
path_page.timeline_6_desc: Transition to complex projects: online stores, educational platforms, corporate sites. Specialization in React app architecture, performance optimization, and SEO.
path_page.skills_desc: Full web development cycle: from prototype to production systems

story_page.title: My story
story_page.subtitle: How a school project led me to building industrial systems and independent web development.
story_page.section_1_text: In 2021 during 11th grade, we did a school Java project — that was my first real programming exposure. By 12th grade, I was self-studying web technologies and digging into C/C++/C#. That's when I knew I wanted to tie my life to development.
story_page.section_2_text: While working at McDonald's in Vienna, in January 2023 I found my first IT job at Schiebel Antriebstechnik. I joined the mobile app team developing for industrial drives — my first commercial project.
story_page.section_3_text: In May 2023, I led Flutter app development for boat motors. The system collected real-time telemetry: speed, GPS, motor characteristics. First project where I handled full development cycle.
story_page.section_4_text: Since October 2023 working on large logistics system for 6 European countries. Here I see how my decisions impact business in real time. Also enrolled at FernUni Hagen to strengthen academic foundation.
story_page.section_5_text: Started freelancing December 2024. First client — father, then through friends and recommendations. Moved from PHP to React realizing modern sites need speed and scalability. Now building complex platforms and e-commerce.
story_page.cta_text: Want to create modern website, mobile app, or optimize logistics? Write me — let's build it together.
